Title: α-Glucosidase I is required for cellulose biosynthesis and morphogenesis in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that form cellulose microfibrils
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key catalytic subunit of cellulose synthase complex; supported as essential for cellulose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Principal structural component of the plant cell wall; required for anisotropic cell expansion and embryo morphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially embryonic hypocotyl epidermal cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling growth at restrictive temperature 31°C accentuates rsw1-1 phenotype; rsw1-2 shows phenotype at all growth temperatures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation in RSW1 strongly reduces crystalline cellulose and causes radial swelling and defective morphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allelic complementation test with rsw1-1; sequencing of mutant allele; embryo morphology analysis; cell wall composition analysis; electron microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Embryo lethality and severe growth defects due to impaired cellulose deposition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a core target for studying and engineering cellulose biosynthesis, but severe loss-of-function causes lethality and major developmental penalties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rsw1-2 is a strong mutant allele of RSW1 with a conserved Gly631Ser substitution near the proposed catalytic site. 2. rsw1-2 embryos and seedlings show radial swelling, thinner cell walls, and major reduction of crystalline cellulose (~22% of wild type). 3. RSW1 is therefore an essential catalytic component required for cellulose biosynthesis and normal embryo morphogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states RSW1 was previously shown to encode a catalytic subunit of cellulose synthase; this study extends phenotype analysis using strong allele rsw1-2.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ER α-glucosidase I; glycoside hydrolase involved in N-glycan processing; single transmembrane domain with ER-lumenal catalytic region
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: N-linked glycan processing required for cellulose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early N-glycan trimming in the endoplasmic reticulum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes the terminal α-1,2-linked glucose from Glc3Man7-9GlcNAc2 during the first step of N-linked glycan trimming
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Triglucosylated N-linked glycan / rhodamine-conjugated triglucoside substrate in assay
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Diglucosylated N-linked glycan after removal of terminal α-1,2-linked glucose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly required for target product by enabling function of other N-glycosylated component(s) needed for cellulose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream enabling step outside direct cellulose polymerization pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for cellulose pathway itself; essential early N-glycan trimming step for cellulose synthesis competence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed throughout plant development; similar low-level expression in inflorescences, stems, rosettes, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Principal structural component of the plant cell wall; required for anisotropic growth and morphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially embryonic epidermal walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of KNF eliminates α-glucosidase I activity, causes severe cellulose deficiency (~13% of wild type), thin disorganized cell walls, and radially swollen embryos
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; multiple mutant allele sequencing; northern blot; PDI mobility shift assay; α-glucosidase I enzyme assay; cell wall composition analysis; Western blot for CESA proteins
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PDI; CESA proteins (tested as non-glycosylated/non-affected); possible downstream dependency on KORRIGAN discussed but not directly tested
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Embryo lethality, severe seedling growth arrest, reduced transmission through pollen, abnormal protein storage bodies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important mechanistic target for understanding glycoprotein-dependent cellulose synthesis, but unsuitable for yield improvement via loss-of-function because defects are lethal/severe
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KNF was map-cloned as At1g67490 and identified as identical to GCS1, encoding α-glucosidase I. 2. knf mutants lack α-glucosidase I activity and show altered N-glycan processing, evidenced by reduced PDI mobility. 3. knf embryos have dramatically reduced crystalline cellulose, demonstrating that N-glycan trimming is required for cellulose biosynthesis, likely through proteins other than CESA catalytic subunits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CESA proteins did not shift after PNGase treatment and accumulated similarly in wild type and knf, arguing KNF affects cellulose synthesis indirectly via other N-glycosylated machinery components.
